title: Peer Universe Identification

firms.inclusion_rationale: World's largest alternative asset manager by AUM; dominant across all major alternative asset classes
Second-largest listed alt manager; dominant credit franchise with $749B in credit AUM; integrated insurance platform via Athene
Iconic PE franchise with ~$750B+ AUM at end-2025; raised record $129B in 2025; diversified into credit and insurance (Global Atlantic)
Pure-play asset management arm spun out from Brookfield Corporation (BN) in Dec 2022; fee-bearing capital of $603B; capital-light fee model
Leading credit-focused alternative manager; 65% of AUM in credit; $622.5B AUM at end-2025, up 29% YoY
One of the original mega-cap PE firms; $477B AUM at end-2025; diversifying into credit and insurance
Fast-growing alt manager focused on permanent capital vehicles; $307B AUM at end-2025, up 52% YoY; GP stakes strategy provides unique diversification
IPO in Jan 2022; $303B AUM at end-2025; differentiated by TPG Rise (impact) and Angelo Gordon credit platform
Largest European-headquartered listed alt manager; EUR 270B AUM (~$285B) at end-2025; ranked #2 PE firm globally by PEI 300
Leading liquid alternatives / quant manager; $227.6B AUM at end-2025, up 35% YoY; includes AHL (systematic) and GLG (discretionary)
Leading private markets firm with direct investing approach; $185B AUM at end-2025, up 21% YoY; strong in evergreen/semi-liquid formats
Private markets solutions provider with $709B total capital responsibility; $189B discretionary AUM; specialist in fund-of-funds and co-investments
Private markets specialist; $138.3B AUM + $819.5B advisory AUM = $957.8B total supervision; competitor to StepStone in PE solutions
Leading European alternative credit manager; $127B AUM at end-2025; strong in structured/mezzanine credit
Global alternatives allocator; $91B AUM at end-2025; 72% of AUM in private asset strategies; specialist in customized portfolios for institutions
European mid-market PE specialist; ~$87B AUM at H1 2025; IPO in 2021; private credit AUM doubled since IPO to EUR 14B
Canadian alt manager with $59.2B AUM; combines third-party fund management with significant proprietary capital deployment
European multi-strategy alt manager; EUR 50.6B AUM (~$54B) at Q1 2025; diversified across credit, real assets, PE

firms.headquarters: George Town, Cayman Islands (operations in Sao Paulo, Brazil)
firms.inclusion_rationale: TARGET COMPANY — Leading Latin American alternative asset manager; $52.6B AUM at end-2025, up 26% YoY; FEAUM of $40.8B; expanding globally via acquisitions
Mid-market PE/VC solutions provider; ~$40B AUM; focus on access-constrained strategies in lower-middle market; recently acquired Stellus Capital
FTSE 100 listed PE/infrastructure investor; GBP 36B PE AUM + GBP 6.9B infra AUM at Sep 2025 (~$43B combined); primarily proprietary capital with growing third-party management
European mid-market specialist; EUR 37.4B AUM (~$40B) at Sep 2025; transitioning from holding company to pure-play third-party asset manager
Pure-play infrastructure manager; EUR 33.3B AUM (~$35B) at end-2025; specialist focus makes it a relevant comp for PAX's infrastructure franchise

Parent of BAM (FIRM-004); includes both asset management fees AND direct asset ownership; ~$1.1T total AUM across ecosystem; classified partial-alt as majority of value is direct asset ownership and insurance, not pure fee-based management

Primarily traditional asset manager; classified partial-alt as majority of $313.8B AUM is in traditional equity/fixed income strategies; alternative investments are a smaller segment; included given MarketVector index inclusion and growing alt exposure

Transitioning from mortgage REIT to diversified alt manager via acquisitions of Sculptor Capital ($37B AUM) and Crestline; $102B total investable assets including $55B third-party AUM; still in transition phase
Major European PE firm; IPO'd on Euronext Amsterdam April 2024; ~EUR 200B AUM (~$215B); FPAUM EUR 148B (~$157B) at end-2025; strong credit, secondaries and infrastructure alongside flagship PE

methodology_notes: Universe compiled from multiple sources: (1) MarketVector Alternative Asset Managers Index (MVAALT) constituents, (2) BlueStar Top 10 US Listed Alternative Asset Managers Index (BUALT), (3) web searches for 'publicly traded alternative asset managers' across US, European, and other exchanges, (4) individual verification of AUM from Q4 2025 / FY 2025 earnings releases and annual reports. AUM figures are as of December 31, 2025 unless otherwise noted. Fee-earning AUM (FEAUM) reported where disclosed. Classification based on estimated revenue mix: pure-play-alt (>=90% from alternatives), majority-alt (60-89%), partial-alt (<60% but meaningful segment). Coverage assessment: strong for US-listed managers, good for UK/European-listed, adequate for Canadian-listed. Latin American coverage relies primarily on PAX as the only significant listed pure-play LatAm alt manager.

Acquired by TD Bank in 2023; no longer independently publicly traded

Not publicly listed; remains a private partnership (portfolio company NOBA Bank IPO'd, but manager itself is private)
